name: Amr ibn Junada
title: The Epic Youth
shortDescription: An 11-year-old boy who went to the battlefield after his father's martyrdom, and his mother threw his severed head back at the enemy.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Junada al-Ansari was an eleven-year-old Khazraji youth who joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Mecca along with his parents. His father, Junada ibn Ka'b, was a sincere Shia and a companion of the Prophet (PBUH) and Amir al-Mu'minin (AS), who was martyred on Ashura morning during the mass attack by Umar Sa'd's army.

After his father's martyrdom, Amr, who was only 11 years old, came to Imam Hussain (AS) and asked for permission to go to the battlefield. The Imam, not wanting this youth's mother to suffer the loss of her son right after losing her husband, said: 'This young man's father has been killed, perhaps his mother would not be pleased if he goes to the battlefield'.

Amr politely replied: 'O son of the Messenger of God! Actually, my mother herself dressed me in battle armor and ordered me to rush to your aid'. Hearing this, Imam Hussain (AS) granted him permission. Amr entered the battlefield holding a sword as tall as himself and recited an amazing war poem.

He chanted in the battlefield: 'My commander is Hussain, and what an excellent commander he is; he is the joy of the heart of the Prophet (PBUH) and Ali (AS)'. Despite his small size, Amr fought bravely until he was martyred by the vindictive Kufan army. His killers were not satisfied with this; they beheaded this child and threw his head towards the tents.

Amr's brave mother picked up the bloody head of her son, wiped the blood from his face, and said: 'Well done, my son! O joy of my heart and light of my eyes!' Then, at the peak of firmness, she powerfully threw her son's head back at the enemy, hitting one of the soldiers and killing him. She then grabbed a tent pole and attacked the enemy until Imam Hussain (AS) brought her back to the tent.
quoteArabic: أَميري حُسَيْنٌ وَنِعْمَ الأَميرُ، سُرورُ فُؤادِ البَشيرِ النَّذيرِ
quote: My commander is Hussain, and what an excellent commander he is; he is the joy of the Prophet's heart.
